sourceRef: tip:Reading a student file in under three minutes

slides.headline: Effortlessly read student files in under 3 minutes
slides.body: Learn a scan pattern for VASCOs to streamline file review.

slides.headline: Identify eligibility
slides.body: Quickly assess if the student meets the eligibility criteria.
bullets: Check if the student is in Chapter 33

slides.headline: Check remaining time
slides.body: Verify the student's eligibility period and remaining time.
bullets: Refer to the student's Chapter 33 enrollment notice

slides.headline: Review holds
slides.body: Identify any holds or issues that might affect the certification.
bullets: Examine the student's last certification letter
Check if there are any outstanding documents

slides.headline: Verify last certification
slides.body: Ensure the student's last certification is current and meets the required criteria.
bullets: Review the student's previous Chapter 33 certification
Check if the student meets the enrollment requirements

slides.headline: Summarize findings
slides.body: Quickly summarize the findings and decide if certification is needed.
bullets: Document the findings in the student's file
Take immediate action based on the summary

slides.body: Try this scan pattern to save time in your VA student file reading. Link in bio.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: Learn a scan pattern to read VA student files in under 3 minutes. Identify eligibility, check remaining time, review holds, and verify the last certification. Get tips to streamline your workflow and save time. Link in bio.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is Step 5, 'Summarize findings,' as it could benefit from a more concise takeaway or a visual element to draw attention to the key action.
